disabled_touchpoints.description: List of touch-point name strings that are entirely blocked. When the touch-point passed into the use case matches any entry in this list, the mapper immediately returns CAN_NOT_OPEN and no offer screen is shown, regardless of any other configuration.

disabled_subtouchpoints.description: List of sub-touch-point name strings that are entirely blocked. When the optional subTouchPoint passed into the use case matches any entry in this list, the mapper immediately returns CAN_NOT_OPEN, preventing the offer screen from showing.

touchpoints.description: List of touch-point configuration entries. Each entry maps one or more touch-point names to an ordered list of offer parameter sets. The mapper finds the first entry matching the requested touch-point, falling back to the entry named 'default' if no specific match exists.

names.description: Additional touch-point name aliases for this entry. The mapper matches a requested touch-point against both the primary 'name' field and every string in this list, allowing multiple distinct touch-points to share the same offer configuration.

name.description: Primary identifier for this touch-point entry. The mapper matches the requested touch-point string against this field (and against the 'names' list). If no named entry matches, the mapper falls back to the entry whose name is 'default'.

info.description: Ordered list of offer parameter entries for this touch-point. The use case iterates through them in order and selects the first entry whose launch-logic conditions all pass. An empty list results in a FAILURE state.

thank_you_popup.description: Identifier for the thank-you popup screen to show after a successful purchase from this offer. An empty value means no thank-you popup is displayed.

launch_logic.description: Ordered list of launch-logic rule objects for this offer entry. The use case evaluates them in order and the offer is eligible if any single rule's conditions all pass. An empty list means the offer is unconditionally eligible.

should_replace_with_ad.description: When true the rule passes only when an interstitial ad is available at this touch-point (checked via AdParamsRepo.canShowInterstitialAd). Replaces the subscription offer with an interstitial ad.

should_replace_with_reward_credit.description: When true the rule passes only when a rewarded-credit ad is available at this touch-point (checked via AdParamsRepo.canShowRewardedAd with the credit flag). Replaces the subscription offer with a rewarded-credit ad opportunity.

install_day_min.description: Minimum number of days since app installation (inclusive) required for this rule to pass. -1 means no lower bound. Compared against the number of days elapsed since the app was first installed.

editor_done_min.description: Minimum number of times the user has completed an editor session (tapped Done) for this rule to pass. -1 means no lower bound. Compared against the persisted editor-done counter.

app_session_end.description: Upper bound (exclusive) for the total app session count. -1 means no upper bound. Together with app_session_start forms a half-open range [start, end) on the app session counter.

editor_done_max.description: Upper bound (exclusive) for the editor-done count. -1 means no upper bound. Together with editor_done_min forms a half-open range [min, max) on the editor-done counter.

session_limit_start.description: Minimum value (inclusive) of how many times this offer screen has been shown (keyed by touch-point plus screen names) for this rule to pass. -1 means no lower bound.

had_subscription_package.description: List of subscription package IDs. The rule passes only when the user's purchase history contains at least one of these package IDs. An empty list means no package-history restriction is applied.

user_had_subscription.description: When true the rule passes only if the user has ever had any subscription; when false only if they have never subscribed. null means subscription history is not checked.

should_replace_with_reward_interstitial.description: When true the rule passes only when a reward-interstitial ad is available at this touch-point (checked via AdParamsRepo.canShowInterstitialAd with the reward-interstitial flag). Combines interstitial availability with a rewarded-ad variant check.

key_session_start.description: Minimum value (inclusive) of the session-key-specific display count for this rule to pass. -1 means no lower bound. Only evaluated when the parent entry's session_key is non-empty.

install_day_max.description: Upper bound (exclusive) for days since app installation. -1 means no upper bound. Together with install_day_min forms a half-open range [min, max) on app age in days.

is_ai_credit.description: When true the rule passes only when the AI credits flow is currently active; when false only when it is inactive. null means the credits flow state is not checked (always passes).

offer_screen_touchpoint.description: Touch-point name used when launching the offer screen from within this rule. Takes precedence over the legacy offer_screen_touch_point field at the rule level.

repeat_key_session.description: If not -1 and session_key is non-empty, the rule passes only when (keySessionCount + 1) % (repeatKeySession + 1) == 0. Controls how often the offer repeats relative to its session-key display count. -1 disables this check.

storage_min_percent.description: Minimum device storage usage percentage (inclusive) required for this rule to pass. -1 means no lower bound. Compared against the cached storage usage percentage from StorageUsageRepository.

is_store_available.description: When true the rule passes only when Google Play Store is the active payment provider (PaymentServiceType.Google is in the active payment types). When false the rule passes only when the Play Store is not active.

key_session_end.description: Upper bound (exclusive) of the session-key-specific display count. -1 means no upper bound. Only evaluated when the parent entry's session_key is non-empty.

repeat_session.description: If not -1, the rule passes only when the total app session count is evenly divisible by this value (appSessionCount % repeatSession == 0). Use this to show the offer every N app sessions. -1 disables this check.

offer_screen_touch_point.description: Legacy field. Touch-point name used when launching the offer screen from within this rule. Superseded by offer_screen_touchpoint at the rule level; used only when the newer field is absent.

restricted_packages.description: List of package/offer ID pairs that restrict this rule to users who are eligible for the specified promotional offers. The rule passes only when the user is eligible for all listed pairs. An empty or absent list means no promotional eligibility restriction.

package_id.description: Subscription package identifier passed to SubscriptionInfoUseCase.isUserEligibleForPromotionalOffer to verify promotional offer eligibility. All entries in the list must pass for the rule to match.

offer_id.description: Offer identifier paired with package_id and passed to SubscriptionInfoUseCase.isUserEligibleForPromotionalOffer. Identifies the specific promotional offer whose eligibility is being checked.

current_tier_types.description: List of subscription tier type values the user must currently be on for this rule to pass. An empty list means any tier is accepted. The special value 'free' matches non-subscribed users. Compared against the tier type of the user's active subscription.

app_session_start.description: Minimum total app session count (inclusive) required for this rule to pass. -1 means no lower bound. Compared against the persisted app session counter.

daily_session_limit.description: Maximum number of times this offer may be shown in a single day. -1 means unlimited. The rule passes only when the current daily display count is strictly less than this value.

should_replace_with_reward.description: When true the rule passes only when a rewarded ad is available at this touch-point (checked via AdParamsRepo.canShowRewardedAd). Effectively gates the offer on rewarded-ad availability.

skip_offer_during_cooldown.description: When true this rule is skipped (does not pass) while the one-day-pass cooldown period is still active, as determined by OneDayPassConfigRepo.isCooldownPeriodPassed. null or false means cooldown state is not evaluated.

session_limit_end.description: Upper bound (exclusive) of the per-touch-point offer display count. -1 means no upper bound. Together with session_limit_start forms a half-open range on the offer session counter.

storage_max_percent.description: Upper bound (exclusive) for device storage usage percentage. -1 means no upper bound. Together with storage_min_percent forms a half-open range [min, max) on storage usage.

offer_variant.description: String identifier for the visual variant of the offer screen to display. Passed through to OfferScreenParam.offerVariant and consumed by the UI layer to select the appropriate screen design. An empty value means the default variant is used.

screens.description: Ordered list of screen name identifiers that make up the offer screen to display when this entry is selected. Also used together with the touch-point name to form the per-screen session-count key for limit tracking.

offer_screen_touchpoint.description: Touch-point name forwarded when opening the offer screen. Takes precedence over the legacy offer_screen_touch_point field. Used by the offer-screen navigation to identify the originating context.

session_key.description: A string key used to track how many times this specific offer has been displayed across sessions, independently of the touch-point. An empty value disables session-key counting and skips key_session_start/key_session_end and repeat_key_session checks.

is_one_time_offer.description: When true this offer entry represents a one-time in-app purchase rather than a recurring subscription. Stored in OneTimePurchaseDetails.isOfferOneTime and used downstream to determine purchase flow type.

offer_screen_touch_point.description: Legacy field. Touch-point name forwarded when opening the offer screen itself. Superseded by offer_screen_touchpoint; used only when offer_screen_touchpoint is absent.
